name: Baseline Security
description: Credential hygiene and dangerous-pattern rules every org should run: hardcoded secrets, credential-bearing URLs, private key material, disabled TLS verification, eval.

policies.name: no-hardcoded-credentials
policies.rule: Never hardcode passwords, secrets, API keys, or tokens as string literals. Load them from environment variables or a secrets manager.
policies.skill: When code needs a credential, read it from process.env / os.environ or the org's secrets manager. Never write the literal value, even as a placeholder that looks real.
policies.fixSuggestion: Move the value to an environment variable or secrets manager and reference it by name.

policies.name: no-credentials-in-urls
policies.rule: Connection strings and URLs must not embed username:password credentials.
policies.skill: Build connection strings from environment variables. Never inline user:pass@host in any scheme (postgres://, mongodb://, https://, redis://).
policies.fixSuggestion: Use a credential-free URL and supply auth via environment variables or a connection config object.

policies.name: no-private-key-material
policies.rule: Private key material must never appear in source code.
policies.skill: Keys live in a secrets manager or key file outside the repo. If a test needs one, generate it at test runtime.
policies.fixSuggestion: Remove the key, rotate it (it is now compromised), and load keys from outside the repository.

policies.name: no-disabled-tls-verification
policies.rule: TLS certificate verification must not be disabled.
policies.skill: If a connection fails TLS verification, fix the certificate chain (add the CA bundle) instead of turning verification off.
policies.fixSuggestion: Re-enable verification and configure the proper CA certificate instead.

policies.name: no-eval
policies.rule: Do not use eval() or equivalent dynamic code execution on strings.
policies.skill: Parse data with JSON.parse or a real parser. Map dynamic behavior through a lookup table of functions instead of evaluating strings.
policies.fixSuggestion: Replace eval with JSON.parse, a parser, or a function lookup table.
